questions.learningObjective: Identify the data elements in an ADS-C report.
questions.fullObjective: Given an ADS-C transmission, identify what surveillance information is contained in the report.

flashcard.front: What information is included in ADS-C surveillance reports to oceanic ATC?
flashcard.back: Aircraft position, altitude, speed, and route intent; ADS-C delivers a contracted surveillance snapshot.

jeopardy.clue: These four core pieces of state data ride in every ADS-C report to oceanic ATC.
choices: Weather radar returns and turbulence
Position, altitude, speed, route intent
Engine parameters and fuel quantity
Cabin temperature and pressure

multipleChoice.question: For oceanic operations, an aircraft forwards ADS-C downlinks to the controlling agency. Which data set is packaged inside those downlinks?
choices: Cabin temperature and pressure data appear in the report.
Position, altitude, speed, and route intent appear in the report.
Engine parameters and fuel quantity appear in the report.
Weather radar returns and turbulence appear in the report.

practice.scenario: You are briefing a new avionics tech on what oceanic ATC actually receives from ADS-C on a Gulfstream G550 crossing the NAT tracks.
practice.question: What information does the report contain?
choices: Cabin temperature and pressure data are transmitted.
Position, altitude, speed, and route intent are transmitted.
Engine parameters and fuel quantity are transmitted.
Weather radar returns and turbulence are transmitted.

preassessment.question: What does an ADS-C report contain for oceanic ATC?
choices: Weather radar returns and turbulence are contained.
Position, altitude, speed, and route intent are contained.
Engine parameters and fuel quantity are contained.
Cabin temperature and pressure data are contained.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the avionics unit that handles CPDLC and ADS-C processing.
questions.fullObjective: Given a list of avionics units, identify which processes CPDLC text messages and ADS-C position reports.

flashcard.front: Which avionics unit processes both CPDLC messages and ADS-C position reports?
flashcard.back: The Communications Management Unit (CMU); it is the datalink router on board.

jeopardy.clue: This onboard datalink router juggles both CPDLC text and ADS-C contracts.
choices: Radio altimeter transceiver
Attitude heading reference system
Communications management unit
Distance measuring equipment

multipleChoice.question: When an aircraft operates in oceanic airspace, which LRU is responsible for handling both CPDLC text exchanges and ADS-C surveillance reporting?
choices: The radio altimeter transceiver processes both.
The attitude heading reference system processes both.
The communications management unit processes both.
The distance measuring equipment processes both.

practice.scenario: During a FANS troubleshoot on a Global 6500, you must identify the single LRU that handles both CPDLC message flow and ADS-C contract reports.
practice.question: Which avionics unit should you check?
choices: The attitude heading reference system is the correct LRU.
The radio altimeter transceiver is the correct LRU.
The communications management unit is the correct LRU.
The distance measuring equipment is the correct LRU.

preassessment.question: Which LRU processes both CPDLC and ADS-C traffic?
choices: The attitude heading reference system processes both.
The radio altimeter transceiver processes both.
The communications management unit processes both.
The distance measuring equipment processes both.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the FAA document providing installation guidance for FANS datalink systems.
questions.fullObjective: Given a FANS datalink installation, identify which FAA Advisory Circular provides installation guidance.

flashcard.front: Which FAA document provides installation guidance for FANS datalink systems?
flashcard.back: FAA Advisory Circular 20-140B, the installation guidance document for FANS and CPDLC systems.

jeopardy.clue: Reach for this AC number when writing an installation data package for a FANS datalink retrofit.
choices: Advisory Circular 43.13-1B
Advisory Circular 20-160
RTCA Document DO-178C
Advisory Circular 20-140B

multipleChoice.question: A shop is performing a FANS datalink retrofit. Which piece of FAA guidance material should the installer reference for approved installation practices?
choices: RTCA Document DO-178C provides FANS install guidance.
Advisory Circular 20-160 provides FANS install guidance.
Advisory Circular 43.13-1B provides FANS install guidance.
Advisory Circular 20-140B provides FANS install guidance.

practice.scenario: You are authoring an installation data package for a FANS 1/A+ retrofit on a Global Express. An auditor asks which AC underpins your installation methods.
practice.question: Which FAA document do you cite?
choices: Advisory Circular 43.13-1B is the correct installation AC.
Advisory Circular 20-160 is the correct installation AC.
RTCA Document DO-178C is the correct installation AC.
Advisory Circular 20-140B is the correct installation AC.

preassessment.question: Which FAA document provides FANS install guidance?
choices: RTCA Document DO-178C provides the guidance.
Advisory Circular 20-160 provides the guidance.
Advisory Circular 43.13-1B provides the guidance.
Advisory Circular 20-140B provides the guidance.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the alerting method required for incoming CPDLC messages.
questions.fullObjective: Given CPDLC installation requirements, identify which alerting method satisfies the incoming-message alert requirement.

flashcard.front: What alerting method satisfies the incoming-CPDLC-message alert requirement?
flashcard.back: An aural tone paired with a visual annunciator; both are required for pilot awareness.

jeopardy.clue: Pair this chime with a lit annunciator and your CPDLC install meets the alerting requirement.
choices: Aural tone with visual annunciator
Message appears on the CDU only
Automatic printout on a cockpit printer
Voice announcement through the speakers

multipleChoice.question: Regulatory guidance mandates a specific crew-alerting implementation whenever a CPDLC uplink arrives. Which of the following approaches meets that mandate?
choices: An aural tone paired with a visual annunciator satisfies it.
Displaying the message on the CDU alone satisfies it.
Automatic printout on a cockpit printer satisfies it.
A voice announcement through the speakers satisfies it.

practice.scenario: You are signing off a CPDLC retrofit on a Legacy 500. The installation must alert the crew when a controller message arrives.
practice.question: Which alerting method satisfies the requirement?
choices: An aural tone combined with a visual annunciator is required.
Displaying the message on the CDU only is required.
Automatic printout on a cockpit printer is required.
A voice announcement through the cockpit speakers is required.

preassessment.question: Which alerting method satisfies the CPDLC incoming-message requirement?
choices: An aural tone with a visual annunciator satisfies it.
Showing the message on the CDU only satisfies it.
Printing the message automatically satisfies it.
Announcing the message by voice satisfies it.

questions.learningObjective: Explain why datalink messages must be formatted to ARINC 622 standards.
questions.fullObjective: Given datalink formatting requirements, explain why ARINC 622 compliance is required for datalink messages.

flashcard.front: Why must datalink equipment format messages to ARINC 622 standards?
flashcard.back: To ensure worldwide ATC interoperability, so any ground center can parse the messages consistently.

jeopardy.clue: This is what ARINC 622 formatting buys you when messages must be parsed by ATC centers worldwide.
choices: Compressed data for bandwidth
Worldwide ATC interoperability
Encrypted data for security
Faster message transmission times

multipleChoice.question: For what reason are datalink messages required to conform to ARINC 622 formatting conventions?
choices: To reduce message transmission time is the purpose.
To ensure worldwide ATC interoperability is the purpose.
To encrypt data for security purposes is the purpose.
To compress data for bandwidth savings is the purpose.

practice.scenario: A design review questions why your FANS software must conform to ARINC 622 message formatting rules.
practice.question: What is the primary reason?
choices: Data compression for bandwidth is the primary reason.
Worldwide ATC interoperability is the primary reason.
Data encryption for security is the primary reason.
Reduced transmission time is the primary reason.

preassessment.question: Why is ARINC 622 message formatting required on datalink gear?
choices: It provides faster message transmission.
It provides worldwide ATC interoperability.
It provides message encryption for security.
It provides message compression for bandwidth.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the source of GPS position data for ADS-C.
questions.fullObjective: Given ADS-C system architecture, identify which component generates the GPS position used in surveillance reports.

flashcard.front: Which component generates the GPS position data for ADS-C surveillance reports?
flashcard.back: A GNSS or GPS satellite receiver; it provides the position fix the CMU uses to build the report.

jeopardy.clue: This receiver tunes in the constellation overhead to supply ADS-C with its position fix.
choices: VOR/DME ground station receiver
Attitude heading reference system
GNSS or GPS satellite receiver
Magnetic flux gate detector

multipleChoice.question: An ADS-C report depends on accurate latitude and longitude values. Which onboard sensor supplies the source positioning information?
choices: The attitude heading reference system generates the position.
A VOR or DME ground station receiver generates the position.
A GNSS or GPS satellite receiver generates the position.
A magnetic flux gate detector generates the position.

practice.scenario: An ADS-C contract on a Challenger 650 is dropping inaccurate position data. You must identify the component that sources the position fix feeding ADS-C.
practice.question: Which component is it?
choices: A magnetic flux gate detector is the source.
The attitude heading reference system is the source.
A GNSS or GPS satellite receiver is the source.
The VOR/DME ground station receiver is the source.

preassessment.question: Which component sources position data for ADS-C reports?
choices: A VOR/DME ground station receiver sources the data.
The attitude heading reference system sources the data.
A GNSS or GPS satellite receiver sources the data.
A magnetic flux gate detector sources the data.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the medium used to transmit ADS-C data to oceanic ATC.
questions.fullObjective: Given transmission options, identify how ADS-C surveillance data reaches the oceanic ATC facility.

flashcard.front: How is ADS-C surveillance data transmitted from aircraft to oceanic ATC?
flashcard.back: Via SatCom or VHF data link contracts, which route the report to the ground network.

jeopardy.clue: ADS-C contracts ride over this SatCom or VHF pipe on their way to oceanic ATC.
choices: The magnetic compass circuit
The onboard Ethernet LAN
HF voice radio relay only
SatCom or VHF data link contracts

multipleChoice.question: By what transmission path do ADS-C surveillance messages travel from the airframe to an oceanic control center?
choices: It is sent over the onboard Ethernet local network.
It is sent through the magnetic compass circuit.
It is sent exclusively via HF radio voice relay.
It is sent via SatCom or VHF data link contracts.

practice.scenario: A customer asks how ADS-C data actually leaves their Global 5500 and reaches Gander Oceanic.
practice.question: How is the data transmitted?
choices: Over the onboard Ethernet network is correct.
Through the aircraft magnetic compass circuit is correct.
Exclusively through HF voice radio relay is correct.
Through SatCom or VHF data link contracts is correct.

preassessment.question: How does ADS-C data reach oceanic ATC?
choices: Through HF voice radio relay only.
Through the aircraft magnetic compass circuit.
Through the onboard Ethernet network.
Through SatCom or VHF data link contracts.

questions.learningObjective: Identify data elements that are not part of an ADS-C report.
questions.fullObjective: Given candidate data elements, identify which item is NOT part of a standard ADS-C position report.

flashcard.front: Which item is NOT a standard data element in an ADS-C report?
flashcard.back: Weather radar return images; ADS-C carries state and intent data, not radar imagery.

jeopardy.clue: ADS-C will never carry these pictures home to ATC, no matter how bad the ride gets.
choices: Weather radar return images
Current aircraft position coordinates
Next waypoint identifier and ETA
Estimated time over future waypoints

multipleChoice.question: Among the following items, identify the one that is NOT carried as a typical field inside an ADS-C report.
choices: Weather radar return images are not a standard element.
Current aircraft position coordinates are a standard element.
Next waypoint identifier and ETA are standard elements.
Estimated times over future waypoints are standard elements.

practice.scenario: A pilot thinks ADS-C sends the radar picture to oceanic ATC. You must correct the misconception by naming what is not in the report.
practice.question: Which item is NOT a standard ADS-C element?
choices: Weather radar return images are not in the report.
Current aircraft position coordinates are not in the report.
Next waypoint identifier and ETA are not in the report.
Estimated times over future waypoints are not in the report.

preassessment.question: Which item is NOT a standard ADS-C position report element?
choices: Weather radar return images are not standard elements.
Current position coordinates are not standard elements.
Next waypoint identifier and ETA are not standard elements.
Estimated times over future waypoints are not standard elements.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the events that trigger ADS-C report transmission.
questions.fullObjective: Given ADS-C operating behavior, identify what triggers position reports to be sent from the aircraft.

flashcard.front: What event triggers ADS-C position reports to be transmitted?
flashcard.back: Contract-based events or periodic intervals; demand, event, and periodic contracts drive the reports.

jeopardy.clue: ADS-C fires off reports for these two kinds of triggers: either the controller's contract or a timer.
choices: Routine voice communication checks
Contract-based events or periodic intervals
Continuous audio feedback detection
Transponder squawk code changes

multipleChoice.question: Under normal operation, what condition causes the FMS to release an ADS-C position update to the ATC ground station?
choices: Transponder squawk code changes trigger reports.
Contract-based events or periodic intervals trigger reports.
Routine voice communication checks trigger reports.
Continuous audio feedback detection triggers reports.

practice.scenario: A new CPDLC/ADS-C user asks when the aircraft actually decides to send ADS-C reports on an oceanic crossing.
practice.question: What event or events trigger the reports?
choices: Continuous audio feedback detection triggers them.
Contract-based events or periodic intervals trigger them.
Routine voice communication checks trigger them.
Transponder squawk code changes trigger them.

preassessment.question: What triggers an ADS-C position report?
choices: Transponder squawk changes trigger reports.
Contract events or periodic intervals trigger reports.
Routine voice comm checks trigger reports.
Continuous audio feedback triggers reports.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the SatCom level required for FANS 1/A operational compliance.
questions.fullObjective: Given FANS compliance requirements, identify which SatCom service level is required for FANS 1/A operations.

flashcard.front: Which SatCom level is required for FANS 1/A operational compliance?
flashcard.back: Level D data-capable SatCom service, which meets FANS 1/A data requirements.

jeopardy.clue: This service level on the SatCom side is the baseline for FANS 1/A data compliance.
choices: Level C basic voice service
Non-TSO uncertified system
Level D data-capable SatCom
ACARS-only text capability

multipleChoice.question: To satisfy FANS 1/A operational requirements, what service class must the installed SatCom terminal support?
choices: ACARS-only text capability is required.
Level C basic voice service is required.
Level D data-capable SatCom is required.
A non-TSO uncertified system is required.

practice.scenario: An operator wants to confirm their SatCom install meets the baseline for FANS 1/A oceanic compliance on a Falcon 900LX.
practice.question: Which SatCom service level is required?
choices: A non-TSO uncertified system meets the requirement.
Level C basic voice service meets the requirement.
Level D data-capable SatCom meets the requirement.
ACARS-only text capability meets the requirement.

preassessment.question: Which SatCom level is required for FANS 1/A compliance?
choices: A non-TSO uncertified system is required.
Level C basic voice service is required.
Level D data-capable SatCom is required.
ACARS-only text capability is required.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the purpose of the external annunciator cube in FANS installs.
questions.fullObjective: Given a FANS installation, identify the purpose of the external annunciator cube.

flashcard.front: What is the purpose of the external annunciator cube installed with FANS equipment?
flashcard.back: It provides a visual alert for CPDLC message receipt so the crew sees when controller text arrives.

jeopardy.clue: That small lit cube on the glareshield does this specific FANS job.
choices: Shows current METAR weather data
Displays the GPS signal quality status
Indicates transponder squawk code changes
Provides a visual alert for CPDLC message receipt

multipleChoice.question: On a FANS-equipped flight deck, why is a standalone annunciator cube wired into the installation?
choices: It indicates when the transponder squawk code changes.
It displays the current GPS signal quality status.
It shows the latest METAR weather data for the route.
It provides a visual alert for CPDLC message receipt.

practice.scenario: A pilot asks what the little lit cube near the glareshield of their FANS-equipped Challenger 605 actually does.
practice.question: What is its purpose?
choices: It shows the latest METAR weather for the destination.
It displays the GPS signal quality status for navigation.
It indicates that the transponder squawk code changed.
It gives the crew a visual alert when a CPDLC message arrives.

preassessment.question: What does the FANS external annunciator cube do?
choices: It shows current METAR weather information.
It displays GPS signal quality status.
It indicates transponder squawk code changes.
It alerts visually when a CPDLC message is received.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the FAA document governing onboard recording of CPDLC communications.
questions.fullObjective: Given regulatory references, identify which FAA document governs the onboard recording requirements for CPDLC communications.

flashcard.front: Which FAA document governs onboard recording requirements for CPDLC communications?
flashcard.back: FAA Advisory Circular 20-160, which addresses CPDLC recording.

jeopardy.clue: This FAA AC is your go-to for how CPDLC messages must be recorded onboard.
choices: FAA Advisory Circular 20-160
RTCA DO-178C software standard
RTCA DO-260 transponder standard
TSO-C132 traffic advisory standard

multipleChoice.question: When CPDLC exchanges must be recorded on the aircraft, which piece of FAA guidance material spells out the retention rules?
choices: FAA Advisory Circular 20-160 governs CPDLC recording.
RTCA DO-178C software standard governs CPDLC recording.
RTCA DO-260 transponder standard governs CPDLC recording.
TSO-C132 traffic advisory standard governs CPDLC recording.

practice.scenario: You are preparing CPDLC recording documentation for an STC package. The FAA wants the controlling AC on the top of the reference list.
practice.question: Which document should you cite?
choices: FAA Advisory Circular 20-160 is the correct reference.
RTCA DO-178C software standard is the correct reference.
RTCA DO-260 transponder standard is the correct reference.
TSO-C132 traffic advisory standard is the correct reference.

preassessment.question: Which FAA document governs CPDLC recording onboard?
choices: FAA AC 20-160 governs it.
RTCA DO-178C governs it.
RTCA DO-260 governs it.
TSO-C132 governs it.
preassessment.explanation: FAA Advisory Circular 20-160 establishes CPDLC onboard recording requirements; the other documents cover unrelated topics.

questions.learningObjective: Distinguish FANS 1/A requirements from other surveillance parameters.
questions.fullObjective: Given parameters, identify which is NOT directly required for successful FANS 1/A data communication.

flashcard.front: Which parameter is NOT directly required for successful FANS 1/A data communication?
flashcard.back: ADS-B Out position broadcast; it is a separate surveillance mandate, not a FANS 1/A datalink requirement.

jeopardy.clue: This surveillance broadcast is its own mandate and does not feed FANS 1/A datalink directly.
choices: CMU CPDLC messaging function
ADS-B Out position broadcast
FMS flight intent data output
SatCom signal quality verification

multipleChoice.question: From the choices below, identify the item that FANS 1/A datalink operations do NOT rely on directly.
choices: SatCom signal quality verification is not directly required.
ADS-B Out position broadcast is not directly required.
CMU CPDLC messaging function is not directly required.
FMS flight intent data output is not directly required.

practice.scenario: An operator insists their ADS-B Out is tied to FANS 1/A. You are reviewing exactly which parameters are required for FANS 1/A data communication.
practice.question: Which parameter is NOT directly required?
choices: FMS flight intent data output is directly required.
ADS-B Out position broadcast is not directly required.
CMU CPDLC messaging function is directly required.
SatCom signal quality is directly required.

preassessment.question: Which parameter is NOT directly required for FANS 1/A comm?
choices: CMU CPDLC messaging is not required.
ADS-B Out position broadcast is not required.
FMS flight intent data output is not required.
SatCom signal quality is not required.

questions.learningObjective: Identify the authority that issues the LOA required for FANS operational approval.
questions.fullObjective: Given FANS operational approval requirements, identify which authority issues the Letter of Authorization.

flashcard.front: Which authority issues the Letter of Authorization required for FANS operational approval?
flashcard.back: The Federal Aviation Administration, which issues the LOA for U.S. operators.

jeopardy.clue: For a U.S. operator, this agency signs the Letter of Authorization that clears the crew for FANS operations.
choices: Eurocontrol air traffic management
International Civil Aviation Organization
Federal Aviation Administration
Original equipment manufacturer

multipleChoice.question: To operate under FANS in US-controlled airspace, an operator must obtain an LOA. What agency grants that authorization?
choices: The original equipment manufacturer issues the LOA.
The International Civil Aviation Organization issues the LOA.
The Federal Aviation Administration issues the LOA.
Eurocontrol air traffic management issues the LOA.

practice.scenario: A Part 91 operator plans to fly the NAT HLA and must obtain a Letter of Authorization for FANS operations on their Gulfstream G450.
practice.question: Which authority issues that LOA?
choices: The original equipment manufacturer issues the LOA.
The International Civil Aviation Organization issues the LOA.
The Federal Aviation Administration issues the LOA.
Eurocontrol air traffic management issues the LOA.

preassessment.question: Which authority issues the FANS LOA?
choices: Eurocontrol air traffic management issues it.
The International Civil Aviation Organization issues it.
The Federal Aviation Administration issues it.
The original equipment manufacturer issues it.

questions.learningObjective: Identify what FMS flight intent data contributes to ADS-C.
questions.fullObjective: Given an ADS-C architecture, identify what information the FMS provides as flight intent data.

flashcard.front: The FMS provides flight intent data to ADS-C. What information does this include?
flashcard.back: Planned route waypoints and timing; the FMS forecasts the intended path and ETAs that ADS-C reports.

jeopardy.clue: This is what the FMS hands off to ADS-C so oceanic ATC knows where you plan to be and when.
choices: Air data computer pressure values
VOR receiver signal strength readings
Transponder Mode S address codes
Planned route waypoints and timing

multipleChoice.question: ADS-C messages incorporate flight intent forwarded by the FMS. What type of data is carried in that intent payload?
choices: Transponder Mode S address codes are provided.
VOR receiver signal strength readings are provided.
Air data computer pressure values are provided.
Planned route waypoints and timing are provided.

practice.scenario: A pilot asks what specifically the FMS hands off to ADS-C to answer controller questions about planned positions and times on a Global 6000.
practice.question: What information is provided?
choices: Transponder Mode S address codes are provided.
VOR receiver signal strength readings are provided.
Air data computer pressure values are provided.
Planned route waypoints and timing are provided.

preassessment.question: What FMS flight intent data goes to ADS-C?
choices: Air data computer pressure values are provided.
VOR receiver signal strength readings are provided.
Transponder Mode S address codes are provided.
Planned route waypoints and timing are provided.
